title: Pointing Out Objects
learningGoal: The learner will write 3 short sentences pointing out physical objects using Kore, Sore, and Are.
instructions: Write three short sentences in Japanese based on the prompts. Pay attention to the physical distance implied in each prompt to choose the correct demonstrative word.

contentItems.text: Remember: Near you = Kore. Near them = Sore. Far away = Are.

writingPrompt: Translate these three sentences: 1) This is a book. 2) That (near you) is an umbrella. 3) That over there is ALSO an umbrella.
promptGoal: To construct 'A wa B desu' sentences using demonstrative pronouns and the 'mo' particle.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: これは ほんです。それは かさです。あれも かさです。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Kore wa hon desu. Sore wa kasa desu. Are mo kasa desu.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: This is a book. That is an umbrella. That over there is also an umbrella.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect translation utilizing Kore, Sore, Are, and the 'mo' particle correctly.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner use Kore, Sore, and Are in the correct order?
Did the learner correctly identify hon (book) and kasa (umbrella)?
Did the learner replace 'wa' with 'mo' in the third sentence?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: これは ほんです。それは かさです。あれも かさです。
acceptableAlternates: Any set of sentences that perfectly mirrors the logic.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: A wa B desu structure applied to Demonstratives.

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic writing! You easily distinguish between Kore, Sore, and Are.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you used the correct demonstrative. Kore (This), Sore (That), Are (That over there).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't accidentally write 'Are wa mo kasa desu'. It should just be 'Are mo kasa desu'.

hintText: For sentence 3, DO NOT write 'Are wa mo'. The 'mo' eats the 'wa'. It is just 'Are mo'.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Kono wa hon desu'. Kono MUST be attached to a noun. Use 'Kore' instead.
